element_scores.name: Dataset Discovery and Identification
element_scores.description: Can a user or system discover and uniquely identify this dataset?

sub_elements.name: Persistent Identifier (DOI, RRID, or URI)
sub_elements.evidence: DOI present: https://doi.org/10.13026/37yb-1t42. ID field present with DOI format.
sub_elements.quality_note: Excellent - DOI is properly formatted and resolves to PhysioNet dataset page.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: DOI format valid (10.13026/37yb-1t42)

sub_elements.name: Dataset Title and Description Completeness
sub_elements.evidence: Title: 'Bridge2AI-Voice - An ethically-sourced, diverse voice dataset linked to health information'. Description: 313 words covering purpose, scope, conditions, methods, release details.
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive title and description with clear project goals, dataset characteristics, and technical details.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Title length: 87 chars. Description length: 1953 chars.

sub_elements.name: Keywords or Tags for Searchability
sub_elements.evidence: 47 keywords covering: voice biomarkers, disease categories (neurological, psychiatric, respiratory, pediatric), acoustic features (MFCC, spectrograms), tools (OpenSMILE, Praat), standards (FAIR, CARE), platforms (PhysioNet).
sub_elements.quality_note: Exceptional keyword coverage with domain-specific terms, conditions, features, tools, and standards.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Keyword count: 47 unique terms

sub_elements.name: Landing Page and Resources (page, hierarchical resources)
sub_elements.evidence: Landing page: https://docs.b2ai-voice.org. Multiple external resources including PhysioNet, GitHub, NIH RePORTER, Zenodo, Health Data Nexus.
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive external resource links including primary documentation, data repositories, GitHub code, and metadata archives.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Page URL valid. External resources: 11 entries with URLs

sub_elements.name: Hierarchical Structure (parent datasets, relationships)
sub_elements.evidence: No parent_datasets or related_datasets fields present. Dataset appears to be standalone without hierarchical linkages.
sub_elements.quality_note: Missing hierarchical structure. Could benefit from linkage to Bridge2AI program-level dataset collection or other Bridge2AI datasets.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Fields parent_datasets and related_datasets not found

element_scores.name: Dataset Access and Retrieval
element_scores.description: Can the dataset and its associated resources be located, accessed, and downloaded?

sub_elements.name: Access Policy and IP Restrictions Defined
sub_elements.evidence: license_and_use_terms present with comprehensive Bridge2AI Voice Registered Access License including: registered access required, DUA signature mandatory, two-year term, no third-party sharing, Certificate of Confidentiality protections.
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive license terms with clear access requirements, restrictions, and legal protections. IP restrictions implied through access control mechanisms.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: license_and_use_terms.license_terms array: 11 entries

sub_elements.name: Regulatory Compliance and Confidentiality Classification
sub_elements.evidence: No regulatory_restrictions, confidentiality_level, hipaa_compliant, other_compliance, or governance_committee_contact fields present.
sub_elements.quality_note: Missing explicit regulatory compliance fields. Dataset involves HIPAA-protected health information (PHI) including voice recordings and EHR data, but no hipaa_compliant field. Certificate of Confidentiality mentioned in narrative but not structured metadata.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Fields not found: regulatory_restrictions, confidentiality_level, hipaa_compliant, other_compliance, governance_committee_contact

sub_elements.name: Download URL or Platform Link Available
sub_elements.evidence: distribution_formats contain access_urls pointing to https://physionet.org/content/b2ai-voice/ for public datasets. Controlled access via DACO@b2ai-voice.org email.
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear access URLs for public dataset via PhysioNet. Controlled access for raw audio through email contact (not downloadable URL).
sub_elements.semantic_validation: access_urls present in distribution_formats (5 entries)

sub_elements.name: Distribution Formats and File Types Specified
sub_elements.evidence: 5 distribution formats: Parquet (spectrograms, MFCCs), TSV (phenotype, static features), Raw audio (controlled access). Detailed descriptions of format structure and contents.
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive format documentation with file types, data structures, and access mechanisms clearly specified.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: distribution_formats: 5 entries with name, description, access_urls

sub_elements.name: Related Datasets and External Resources Linked
sub_elements.evidence: 11 external resources: PhysioNet landing page, project documentation, GitHub repository, NIH RePORTER, Health Data Nexus, Zenodo archive, publication DOI, DACO contact, Bridge2AI Program, b2aiprep library.
sub_elements.quality_note: Excellent coverage of external resources including data repositories, code repositories, funding information, publications, and related platforms.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: external_resources: 11 entries with URLs/DOIs

element_scores.name: Data Reuse and Interoperability
element_scores.description: Is sufficient information provided to reuse and integrate the dataset with others?

sub_elements.name: License Terms Allow Reuse
sub_elements.evidence: License allows research use with registered access and DUA. Public disclosure of results encouraged in open-access journals. Recognition of data source required in publications.
sub_elements.quality_note: License permits research reuse with appropriate attribution and DUA compliance. Clear terms for publication and data source recognition.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: license_and_use_terms contains reuse terms

sub_elements.name: Data Formats Are Standardized (encoding, format)
sub_elements.evidence: Standard formats: Parquet (for array data), TSV (for tabular data), JSON (for data dictionaries). Common in data science ecosystems.
sub_elements.quality_note: Excellent use of standardized, interoperable formats (Parquet, TSV, JSON) compatible with Python, R, and data science tools.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Formats: Parquet, TSV, JSON identified in distribution_formats

sub_elements.name: Schema or Ontology Conformance Stated
sub_elements.evidence: No conforms_to or conforms_to_schema fields present. No explicit schema conformance declarations.
sub_elements.quality_note: Missing schema conformance metadata. Dataset could reference D4D schema, FHIR for health data, or domain ontologies (SNOMED CT for conditions).
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Fields conforms_to and conforms_to_schema not found

sub_elements.name: Variable Metadata with Identifiers Defined
sub_elements.evidence: No variables field present. Data dictionaries mentioned (phenotype.json, static_features.json) but not represented as structured variable metadata.
sub_elements.quality_note: Missing structured variable metadata. JSON data dictionaries exist but are external files, not schema-defined variable metadata with identifiers.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Field variables not found

sub_elements.name: Use Guidance Provided (intended, prohibited uses)
sub_elements.evidence: 5 intended_uses covering AI/ML development, acoustic biomarker research, education, multimodal research, ethical AI modeling. 4 discouraged_uses covering clinical decision-making without validation, re-identification attempts, consent violations, surveillance technologies.
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive use guidance with clear intended and discouraged uses. Addresses ethical considerations, clinical validation requirements, and privacy protections.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: intended_uses: 5 entries, discouraged_uses: 4 entries (no prohibited_uses)

element_scores.name: Ethical Use and Privacy Safeguards
element_scores.description: Does the dataset provide clear information about consent, privacy, and ethical oversight?

sub_elements.name: IRB or Ethics Review and Data Protection Impact
sub_elements.evidence: human_subject_research present with University of South Florida IRB approval. irb_approval and ethics_review_board fields populated. No data_protection_impacts field.
sub_elements.quality_note: IRB approval documented. Missing formal data protection impact assessment (DPIA) as structured metadata, though privacy considerations discussed narratively.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: human_subject_research.irb_approval: present. data_protection_impacts: not found

sub_elements.name: Deidentification Method Described
sub_elements.evidence: Cleaning strategy cleaning-001 describes HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification: 18 identifier categories removed, geographic data limited to country, date precision limited to year.
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive de-identification methodology following HIPAA Safe Harbor standards. Detailed listing of removed identifier categories.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: is_deidentified field not present, but deidentification described in cleaning_strategies

sub_elements.name: Privacy Protections and Re-identification Risk Assessment
sub_elements.evidence: 4 sensitive_elements entries describing: voice as biometric identifier (raw audio controlled access), EHR data protections, demographic de-identification, Certificate of Confidentiality protections. No explicit reidentification_risk field.
sub_elements.quality_note: Strong privacy protections documented. Missing formal re-identification risk assessment as structured metadata.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: sensitive_elements: 4 entries. participant_privacy: not found. reidentification_risk: not found

sub_elements.name: Informed Consent Obtained from Participants
sub_elements.evidence: human_subject_research describes written informed consent for data collection initiative and data sharing. Consent process includes authorization for voice data collection, EHR access, and research data sharing.
sub_elements.quality_note: Informed consent documented with clear scope (data collection, EHR access, sharing). Missing structured informed_consent field with consent type details.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Informed consent described in human_subject_research narrative. informed_consent field: not found

sub_elements.name: Vulnerable Populations and Compensation Documented
sub_elements.evidence: Pediatric cohort (subpop-005) identified as requiring additional ethical considerations and privacy precautions. Pediatric data not yet released (v1.1) pending additional safeguards. No participant_compensation field.
sub_elements.quality_note: Vulnerable population (pediatric) explicitly recognized with additional protections. Missing structured vulnerable_populations field and participant compensation details.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Pediatric cohort identified in subpopulations. vulnerable_populations and participant_compensation fields: not found

element_scores.name: Data Composition and Structure
element_scores.description: Can the dataset's structure, modality, and population be understood from metadata?

sub_elements.name: Cohort or Subpopulations Characteristics Described
sub_elements.evidence: 5 subpopulations: Voice Disorders, Neurological/Neurodegenerative, Mood/Psychiatric, Respiratory, Pediatric. Each with detailed condition descriptions.
sub_elements.quality_note: Excellent subpopulation documentation with disease-specific cohorts and clinical characteristics.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: subpopulations: 5 entries with clinical descriptions

sub_elements.name: Number of Instances or Samples Reported
sub_elements.evidence: instance-001 reports 306 participants with 12,523 recordings. Multi-institutional enrollment across five sites. v1.0 release statistics provided.
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear instance counts for participants and recordings. Version-specific statistics documented.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: instances: 1 entry with participant and recording counts

sub_elements.name: Variable-Level Metadata, Tabular Flag, and Data Splits
sub_elements.evidence: No variables, is_tabular, is_data_split, or is_subpopulation fields present. Data dictionaries mentioned (phenotype.json, static_features.json) but not as structured metadata.
sub_elements.quality_note: Missing structured variable metadata and dataset structure flags. Dataset contains tabular data (phenotype.tsv, static_features.tsv) but is_tabular flag not set.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Fields not found: variables, is_tabular, is_data_split, is_subpopulation

sub_elements.name: Data Topics or Conditions Represented
sub_elements.evidence: Comprehensive condition coverage documented in keywords and subpopulations: voice disorders (laryngeal cancer, vocal fold paralysis), neurological (Parkinson's, Alzheimer's, ALS, stroke), psychiatric (depression, schizophrenia, bipolar), respiratory (COPD, pneumonia), pediatric (autism, speech delay).
sub_elements.quality_note: Exceptional documentation of represented conditions across 5 disease categories with specific diagnoses listed.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: Conditions documented in keywords (47 terms) and subpopulations (5 cohorts)

sub_elements.name: Data Quality, Anomalies, and Missing Data Documented
sub_elements.evidence: sampling_strategies describes non-random, non-representative targeted recruitment. No explicit anomalies or missing_data_documentation fields, but quality considerations implied through standardized protocols.
sub_elements.quality_note: Sampling methodology documented with representativeness assessment. Missing explicit anomalies and missing data documentation.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: sampling_strategies: 1 entry. anomalies and missing_data_documentation: not found

sub_elements.name: Dataset Version Number Provided
sub_elements.evidence: No version field present at dataset level. Version information exists in updates description (v1.0, v1.1, v2.0.0, v2.0.1) but not as top-level version field.
sub_elements.quality_note: Version information documented in update history but missing top-level version field. Current version unclear without reading full update narrative.

sub_elements.evidence: D4D file header includes generation metadata: 'Generation Method: Claude Code Agent Deterministic', source file (VOICE_preprocessed.txt, 89K, 9 source files), schema source. Collection mechanisms describe data flow from REDCap to b2aiprep conversion.

sub_elements.evidence: 6 tasks covering: voice disorder AI/ML models, neurological disorder detection, mood/psychiatric disorder algorithms, respiratory screening, pediatric disorder models, workforce development/education.

element_scores.name: Technical Transparency (Data Collection and Processing)
element_scores.description: Can data collection and processing steps be replicated or understood?

sub_elements.name: Collection Mechanisms and Settings Described
sub_elements.evidence: 3 collection_mechanisms: custom smartphone app with tablet/headset, multi-institutional 5-site enrollment (2022-2026), standardized protocol with demographics/questionnaires/voice recordings. IRB-approved protocols.
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive collection mechanism documentation with technology, sites, timeframes, and protocols detailed.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: collection_mechanisms: 3 entries with detailed descriptions

sub_elements.name: Data Acquisition Methods Listed
sub_elements.evidence: 3 acquisition_methods: voice recording tasks (sustained phonation, respiratory sounds, cough, free speech), self-reported questionnaires (demographics, disease-specific, confounders), EHR access for gold standard validation.
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear acquisition methods covering voice data, self-reports, and clinical validation sources.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: acquisition_methods: 3 entries with detailed descriptions

sub_elements.name: Preprocessing, Cleaning, Labeling, and Annotation Quality
sub_elements.evidence: 7 preprocessing_strategies: audio conversion (monaural, 16kHz, Butterworth filter), spectrogram extraction (FFT parameters), MFCC extraction (60 coefficients), OpenSMILE features, Praat/Parselmouth features, Whisper transcription, REDCap export via b2aiprep. 3 cleaning_strategies: HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification, privacy protections (audio omission, transcript removal), multi-site standardization.
sub_elements.quality_note: Exceptional technical transparency with detailed preprocessing parameters, software tools, and cleaning procedures. No explicit annotation_analyses or machine_annotation_tools fields.
sub_elements.semantic_validation: preprocessing_strategies: 7 entries. cleaning_strategies: 3 entries. labeling_strategies, annotation_analyses, machine_annotation_tools: not found

sub_elements.name: Software and Tools Documented
sub_elements.evidence: Software tools mentioned: OpenSMILE (acoustic features), Parselmouth/Praat (phonetic/prosodic features), OpenAI Whisper Large (transcription), b2aiprep (REDCap conversion), REDCap (data management). No structured software_and_tools field.
sub_elements.quality_note: Good software documentation integrated into preprocessing descriptions. Missing structured software_and_tools field with versions and citations.

sub_elements.evidence: 11 external_resources including code repositories (GitHub, b2aiprep), data platforms (PhysioNet, Health Data Nexus), documentation, publications. Standards implied (FAIR, CARE principles in keywords). No conforms_to or imputation_protocols fields.

issues_detected.description: Missing hierarchical dataset relationships (parent_datasets, related_datasets) for connecting to Bridge2AI program and consortium datasets
issues_detected.recommendation: Add related_datasets entries for other Bridge2AI datasets (AI-READI, CM4AI, CHORUS) with typed relationships (partOf, relatedTo). Add parent reference to Bridge2AI program-level collection.

issues_detected.description: Missing regulatory compliance and governance fields despite sensitive health data
issues_detected.recommendation: Add hipaa_compliant field (value: compliant with HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification). Add confidentiality_level classification. Add governance_committee_contact for DACO.
Add conforms_to entries for: D4D schema, FAIR principles, CARE principles, Bridge2AI standards. Add conforms_to_schema for data dictionaries (phenotype.json, static_features.json).

issues_detected.description: Missing structured variable metadata despite tabular datasets
issues_detected.recommendation: Add variables array with metadata for key phenotype and feature variables. Set is_tabular to true. Reference external data dictionaries (phenotype.json, static_features.json).

issues_detected.description: Missing top-level version field for current dataset version
issues_detected.recommendation: Add version field at root level with current version number (e.g., 'v2.0.1' or '2.0.1').

issues_detected.description: Missing limitations, biases, and anomalies documentation required for responsible AI
issues_detected.recommendation: Add known_limitations covering: non-representative sampling, adult-only data (pediatric pending), specialty clinic recruitment, multi-session variability. Add known_biases with BiasTypeEnum categories: selection_bias (specialty clinic recruitment), demographic_bias (institutional populations). Add anomalies documenting data quality issues.

issues_detected.description: Missing formal provenance and sustainability metadata
issues_detected.recommendation: Add was_derived_from for source provenance. Add raw_data_sources documenting original data collection. Add publisher field identifying PhysioNet as publishing platform.
Add citation field with formatted citation string (DataCite or BibTeX format) and citation instructions for proper attribution.

issues_detected.description: Missing structured software and tools metadata
issues_detected.recommendation: Add software_and_tools array documenting: OpenSMILE (version, URL), Praat/Parselmouth (versions), OpenAI Whisper Large, b2aiprep (version, GitHub), REDCap. Include software citations where available.

Migrate ethics information from narratives to structured fields: informed_consent (consent type, scope), participant_privacy (protection methods), vulnerable_populations (pediatric cohort details), participant_compensation (if applicable), data_protection_impacts (DPIA results), reidentification_risk (assessment).

issues_detected.description: Deidentification described in cleaning_strategies but no is_deidentified field
issues_detected.recommendation: Add is_deidentified class with deidentification method, standard (HIPAA Safe Harbor), and risk assessment.

passed: URL format valid for all external_resources and access_urls
Participant counts consistent between instance-001 and description (306 participants, 12,523 recordings)
Version history chronologically ordered (v1.0 → v1.1 → v2.0.0 → v2.0.1)
Funding period consistent with data collection period (2022-2026)
Distribution formats align with preprocessing outputs (spectrograms→Parquet, phenotype→TSV)
Subpopulation cohorts match disease categories in keywords and purposes
warnings: Version information in updates but no top-level version field - current version unclear without reading narrative
Software tools mentioned in preprocessing descriptions but no structured software_and_tools array with versions
Data dictionaries (phenotype.json, static_features.json) mentioned but not represented as structured variables metadata
Ethics protections described narratively but missing structured fields (informed_consent, participant_privacy, etc.)
Pediatric cohort identified as vulnerable population but no formal vulnerable_populations field

semantic_analysis.semantic_insights: The VOICE D4D datasheet demonstrates strong documentation quality with comprehensive coverage of motivation (purposes, tasks, addressing_gaps), composition (instances, subpopulations, sampling), collection (mechanisms, acquisition, preprocessing), distribution (formats, access, licensing), and maintenance (updates, maintainers). Key strengths include: (1) exceptional keyword coverage (47 terms) spanning conditions, features, tools, and standards; (2) detailed preprocessing transparency with 7 strategies documenting technical parameters (FFT window sizes, MFCC coefficients, sampling rates); (3) comprehensive ethical considerations with IRB approval, HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification, and Certificate of Confidentiality protections; (4) clear access control model distinguishing public (derived features via PhysioNet DUA) vs. controlled access (raw audio via DACO); (5) well-documented funding with grant numbers, amounts, and program context; (6) extensive external resources linking to documentation, code, data, and publications. Primary gaps are in FAIR/RAI alignment metadata: missing schema conformance declarations (conforms_to), structured variable metadata (variables), hierarchical dataset relationships (related_datasets to other Bridge2AI datasets), formal limitations/biases documentation required for responsible AI (known_limitations, known_biases with BiasTypeEnum taxonomy), regulatory compliance fields (hipaa_compliant, confidentiality_level), and provenance metadata (was_derived_from, publisher, citation). The dataset excels at technical transparency but could strengthen interoperability through standards conformance and responsible AI through structured bias/limitations disclosure.

recommendations: HIGH PRIORITY: Add known_limitations documenting: non-representative sampling (specialty clinic recruitment), adult-only data release (pediatric pending), institutional population constraints, multi-session variability, condition coverage gaps

HIGH PRIORITY: Add known_biases with BiasTypeEnum categorization: selection_bias (specialty clinic recruitment, targeted disease cohorts), demographic_bias (institutional populations, geographic concentration), measurement_bias (device variability across sites)

HIGH PRIORITY: Add regulatory compliance fields: hipaa_compliant (compliant), confidentiality_level (controlled_access_required), governance_committee_contact (DACO), other_compliance (Certificate of Confidentiality)
MEDIUM PRIORITY: Add hierarchical dataset relationships: related_datasets linking to AI-READI, CM4AI, CHORUS (Bridge2AI consortium datasets); parent_datasets linking to Bridge2AI program collection
